Contextual Diff Lens: for the first diverging step, expand the trace to reveal the earliest upstream message (user input, tool output, or prior agent response) that differed between runs, along with a side-by-side token-level diff of that message.

Chaos Puppetry: inject synthetic failures and edge-case inputs into a recorded multi-agent trace, then replay step-by-step to observe recovery and handoff behavior.

Canary-Leak Detector: injects unique canary strings into each agent’s private context before a test run, then scans all inter-agent messages and final outputs for those canaries to identify unauthorized information flows, generating an audit-ready report of privacy violations.

Agentic Counterfactual Playback: a debugging tool that records all inter-agent messages, allows developers to pause at any step, inject an alternative agent response, and simulates the new trajectory, displaying a diff of the resulting conversation tree and final outputs to illuminate causal dependencies and edge-case behavior.
